data.title: Exyn Technologies, Autonomous Robotics and 3D Mapping, Files for Nasdaq IPO
data.description:
Exyn Technologies filed for an initial public offering, seeking to list its common stock and warrants on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbols EXYN and EXYNW. The Philadelphia-based company develops autonomous robotics and AI software for industrial and defense applications, focusing on GPS-denied environments. While pricing terms were not disclosed, Exyn plans to use proceeds for growth, working capital, and to repay debt.

Business Description

Exyn Technologies develops autonomy software and systems that enable aerial and ground robots to navigate and collect data in complex, GPS-denied environments. Its core platform, ExynAI, delivers simultaneous localization and mapping (SLAM), motion planning, and control, providing real-time, survey-grade 3D mapping and autonomous navigation. The company packages its software within Nexys, a modular mapping and autonomy payload that can be mounted on drones, ground robots, handheld rigs, or vehicles, and ExynView, a planning and post-processing suite for mission control and digital twin creation.

Exyn generates revenue from hardware-enabled software sales, software licensing, services, and support. Its solutions target mining, construction, infrastructure inspection, geospatial, energy, and defense markets, emphasizing safety, speed of data capture, and operational efficiency. Competitive strengths include Level 4B autonomy in GPS-denied environments, hardware-agnostic integration via API/SDK, and a proven field track record across thousands of autonomous missions.

Market Overview

  • Total addressable market: Commercial drone market projected to reach $54.6 billion by 2030 (Grand View Research).
  • Market growth: Unmanned maritime systems expected to grow from $4.9 billion to $16.0 billion by 2033 (CAGR ~14.3%).
  • Market position: Provider of Level 4B autonomy and hardware-agnostic payloads for GPS-denied operations.
  • Key competitors: Emesent, Skydio, Anduril, Trimble, Hexagon/Leica, Faro, Navvis, Flyability, Boston Dynamics, Built Robotics; defense primes such as Lockheed Martin and Northrop Grumman.
  • Industry trends: Accelerating adoption of autonomy, digitization and digital twins, BVLOS regulatory progress, and increased defense demand for autonomous ISR.

Operational Metrics

  • Customers: 49 customers in 2025, including 24 mining, 22 geospatial, and 3 government-related entities
  • Locations: Headquarters in Philadelphia, PA; subsidiary in Chile (Exyn Latin America SPA) and defense-focused subsidiary (Exyn Defense, Inc.)
  • Geographic presence: Sales in the U.S., Canada, Australia, and other international markets
  • Partnerships: Channel partners generated approximately 42% of 2025 sales; integration partnerships via Nexys API and ExynAI SDK
  • Distribution: Distributors include World Class Mining Services Mexico and C.R. Kennedy & Company; channel accounts for ~42% of sales
  • Customer concentration: Top five customers accounted for ~45% of 2025 revenue; government customers ~4%
  • Revenue by geography (2025): U.S. ~33%, Canada ~18%, Australia ~11%, Other ~38%

Financials Highlights

  • Revenue (current): $5,810,504 (2025)
  • Revenue growth: 4.4% year-over-year
  • Gross profit: $2,090,281 (36% margin)
  • Operating income: $(9,656,500)
  • Net income: $(12,191,687)

Management

  • Brandon Torres Declet, Chief Executive Officer - Five-time CEO/founder with leadership roles at Measure, AgEagle, and Unusual Machines; appointed to the FAA’s Advanced Aviation Advisory Committee.
  • Ricardo Sotelo, Chief Financial Officer - Former finance leader at Mint House and Knotel; prior roles at ViacomCBS, Microsoft, and American Express; Wharton MBA.
  • Benjamin Williams, Chief Operating Officer - Background in product and operations at AT&T/Fullscreen; co-founded startups including Reelio; U.S. Navy and Lockheed Martin experience.
  • Brandon Duick, Chief Technology Officer - Robotics engineer with deep experience in SLAM, LiDAR calibration, and autonomy systems; previously at Boeing.
  • Vanessa Varian, Chief Marketing Officer - Technology marketing and growth leader with IPO readiness and corporate communications experience.

IPO Structure

  • Issuer: Exyn Technologies, Inc.
  • Filing date: March 19, 2026
  • Proposed ticker: EXYN
  • Exchange: Nasdaq
  • Lead underwriters: Lucid Capital Markets, LLC
  • Use of proceeds: Growth capital, working capital, general corporate purposes, and repayment of indebtedness (Western Alliance Bank $3.5m, Neolync Convertible Note $1.5m, Maximcash Loan $0.6m, NCH Convertible Note $0.75m, Neolync Term Loan $1.5m).

Original SEC Filing: Exyn Technologies, Inc. - S-1 - Mar. 19, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: US judges speak out against threats, mysterious pizza deliveries
data.description:
Key points:
  • Judges face rising threats, including emails and mysterious pizza deliveries
  • Judicial ethics opinion allows advocacy for security and independence
  • D.C.-based judge speaks of threats after ruling against Trump policy

By Nate Raymond

A group of federal judges on Thursday publicly denounced a rise in threats against them and their colleagues, including hateful messages aimed at a judge who ruled against part of President Donald Trump's hardline immigration agenda.

Five judges appointed by both Democratic and Republican presidents during a virtual event recounted receiving disturbing emails wishing them dead and mysterious pizza deliveries to their private homes as reasons why they could not remain silent.

"Staying quiet is no longer an option," Miami-based U.S. District Judge Beth Bloom said.

They spoke just days after U.S. Supreme Court Chief Justice John Roberts on Tuesday spoke out against hostility directed at judges in personal terms, saying it "is dangerous and ​it's got to stop."

The judges echoed Roberts' remarks during an event hosted by Speak Up for Justice, which advocates for an independent judiciary. Some said they felt freer to speak now after a judicial ethics opinion last month advised they could defend colleagues against "illegitimate" attacks and criticism.

They argued more needed to be done to promote civilized behavior and encouraged bar associations to step up to condemn personal attacks on judges, which they said threaten to undermine judicial independence.

U.S. District Judge Ana Reyes recounted how she became the subject of countless "threatening and problematic" voicemails and emails after she blocked the Trump administration in February from ending legal protections for more than 350,000 Haitian migrants.

"I hope you lose your life by lunchtime, you worthless whore," one email said, according to Reyes, who previously recounted the message at a court hearing.

Reyes, who was appointed by Democratic President Joe Biden, said some also targeted her for being gay. The Uruguayan-born judge said the "most painful" threats came from individuals who said "you're being un-American, you're being undemocratic, or you're an immigrant and should go back to your country."

Such threats have been on the rise, according to the U.S. Marshals Service, which in the 2025 fiscal year that ended September 30 recorded 564 threats to 396 judges. So far this year, it has tracked 241 threats to 202 judges.

"The threats are real and they continue," said U.S. District Judge Mark Norris, a Trump appointee in Memphis, Tennessee.

The judge in an October ruling criticized federal authorities for doing little to investigate the shooting of one of his law clerks, which occurred days after the 2024 verdict in a trial of former police officers charged in relation to the beating death of Tyre Nichols, an unarmed Black motorist.

Norris said he, like many other judges recently, had also received an unsolicited pizza delivered to his home in the name of U.S. District Judge Esther Salas' son, who was fatally shot in 2020 by a disgruntled lawyer who came to her New Jersey home.

Judges say those pizza deliveries appear aimed at intimidating and are viewed as a we-know-where-you-live warning.

"I really have never eaten pizza since, and I feel healthier for it," Norris said.

Read more:

US Supreme Court's Roberts says personal hostility aimed at judges has 'got to stop'

US judges may speak out against 'illegitimate' criticism, ethics panel says

US judges recount death threats, 'swatting' after rulings against Trump

These judges ruled against Trump. Then their families came under attack.

data.title: Nineteen-year-old Mexican man dies in ICE custody, agency says
data.description:
By Jasper Ward

A 19-year-old man died at a federal detention center on Monday, according to U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement, the youngest known person to die in federal immigration custody during the second Trump administration.

ICE identified the man as Royer Perez Jimenez, of Mexico, who was being held at the Glades County Detention Center in Moore Haven, Florida. His death raised to at least 13 the number of immigrants to die in federal immigration custody this year.

Perez Jimenez's death is a presumed suicide, ICE said, adding that the official cause of death is under investigation after he was found unresponsive in the early hours of the morning.

The detention center's staff unsuccessfully tried to resuscitate him for nearly 10 minutes after he was found, ICE said.

The agency said Perez Jimenez was in custody after being arrested and charged with felony fraud for impersonating and resisting an officer.

He had initially entered the U.S. in 2022, was returned home after an encounter with the U.S. Border Patrol, and later reentered the U.S. illegally on an unknown date, ICE said.

"ICE is committed to ensuring that all those in custody reside in safe, secure and humane environments. Comprehensive medical care is provided from the moment individuals arrive and throughout the entirety of their stay," the agency said in a statement on Wednesday.

ICE said 31 deaths - a two-decade high - were recorded among immigrants in federal custody all of last year, the first year of U.S. President Donald Trump's second White House term.

data.title: Las Vegas Sands Chairman & CEO Sold Shares Worth Over $3.2M
data.description:
Patrick Dumont, a Director and Chairman & CEO, reported a direct sale of common stock on March 17, 2026. The shares were sold at a weighted average price of $54.75 per share. The total sale amount was $3,294,033. Following the transaction, Mr. Dumont directly owns 511,942 shares of common stock. The reporting person exercised options with an expiration date of March 28, 2026 and sold underlying shares to cover the exercise price and related tax obligations, while retaining the remaining shares received. Mr. Dumont holds options to purchase 1,500,000 vested shares and 203,690 unvested restricted stock units.

data.title: Canada's Glacier Media 2025 revenue falls 3.1%
data.description:
Overview

  • Canada business information publisher's 2025 revenue fell 3.1% as legacy print operations declined

  • EBITDA dropped to C$7.5 mln, driven by lower revenue in core operations

  • Advertising revenue declined, partly offset by growth in data and subscription revenue

Outlook

  • Company did not provide specific guidance for future periods

Result Drivers

  • PRINT OPERATIONS DECLINE - Revenue fell as legacy print operations were sold or closed by end of 2025

  • ADVERTISING WEAKNESS - Advertising revenue decreased due to market uncertainty and closures or sales of community media publications

  • DATA AND SUBSCRIPTION GROWTH - Data and subscription revenue increased, mainly in environmental risk and compliance information operations

data.title: FedEx Q3 Profit Rises
data.description:
MEMPHIS (dpa-AFX) - FedEx Corp. (FDX) Thursday reported third-quarter net income of $1.06 billion or $4.41 per share, compared to $0.91 billion or $3.76 per share last year.

Adjusted net income for the quarter was $1.26 billion or $5.25 per share, compared to $1.09 billion or $4.51 per share last year.

Revenues for the quarter were $24.0 billion, compared to $22.2 billion last year.

FedEx is revising its fiscal 2026 outlook, and now expects a 6.0% to 6.5% revenue growth rate year over year, compared to the prior forecast of 5% to 6% growth. Earnings per share is expected to be $16.05 to $16.85 before the MTM

retirement plans accounting adjustments compared to the prior forecast

of $14.80 to $16.00.

data.title: AVALON HOLDINGS CORPORATION ANNOUNCES 2025 FULL YEAR AND FOURTH QUARTER RESULTS
data.description:

(21:05 GMT) *Avalon Holdings 4Q Loss/Shr 9c >AWX
(21:05 GMT) *Avalon Holdings 4Q Rev $21.5M >AWX
(21:05 GMT) Press Release: AVALON HOLDINGS CORPORATION ANNOUNCES 2025 FULL YEAR AND FOURTH QUARTER RESULTS

WARREN, Ohio, March 19, 2026 /PRNewswire/ — Avalon Holdings Corporation (NYSE Amex:AWX) today announced financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year ended December 31, 2025.

Net operating revenues in the fourth quarter of 2025 were $21.5 million compared with $17.6 million in the fourth quarter of 2024. The Company recorded a net loss attributable to Avalon Holdings Corporation common shareholders of $0.4 million in the fourth quarter of 2025 compared with a net loss attributable to Avalon Holdings Corporation common shareholders of $0.5 million in the fourth quarter of 2024. For the fourth quarter of 2025, basic net loss per share attributable to Avalon Holdings Corporation common shareholders was $0.09 compared with basic net loss per share attributable to Avalon Holdings Corporation common shareholders of $0.13 in the fourth quarter of 2024.

For the year ended December 31, 2025, net operating revenues were $83.6 million compared with $83.8 million for the year ended December 31, 2024. The Company recorded a net income attributable to Avalon Holdings Corporation common shareholders of $0.3 million for the year ended December 31, 2025 compared with a net income attributable to Avalon Holdings Corporation common shareholders of $1.3 million for the year ended December 31, 2024. For the year ended December 31, 2025, basic net income per share attributable to Avalon Holdings Corporation common shareholders was $0.08 compared with basic net income per share attributable to Avalon Holdings Corporation common shareholders of $0.34 for year ended December 31, 2024.

Avalon Holdings Corporation provides waste management services to industrial, commercial, municipal and governmental customers in selected northeastern and midwestern U.S. markets, captive landfill management services and salt water injection well operations. Avalon Holdings Corporation also owns Avalon Resorts and Clubs Inc., which includes the operation of a hotel and its associated resort amenities, four golf courses and related country clubs and a multipurpose recreation center.

data.title: NovaBay Pharmaceuticals 10-K: $0.6M Revenue, $(3.80) EPS, heavy net loss
data.description:
NovaBay Pharmaceuticals reported consolidated net sales of $0.6 million for the year, with basic loss per share of $(3.80) driven by a consolidated net loss of $(22.14) million. The company recorded an operating loss of $(8.44) million for continuing operations while discontinued operations (Avenova and PhaseOne) contributed combined sales and positive results that partially offset continuing‑operations losses. These results reflect the company's mix of continuing operations losses and one-time or discontinued‑operations income reported in the Form 10‑K.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue (Net sales): $0.6 million (discontinued operations combined sales: Avenova $0.433M; PhaseOne $0.122M)
  • Gross profit: $0.3 million (discontinued operations: Avenova gross profit $0.263M; PhaseOne $0.047M)
  • Operating income (loss): $(8.44) million (Operating loss $(8,439)K for continuing operations)
  • Net income (loss): $(22.14) million (Consolidated net loss $(22,141)K; continuing operations net loss $(33,222)K offset by discontinued operations income $11,081K)
  • Basic (loss) earnings per share attributable to common stockholders: $(3.80) per share

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and segment activity: Reported combined sales from discontinued product lines Avenova and PhaseOne, which produced the majority of the reported net sales for the period.
  • Channel shift: Accelerated digital and direct‑to‑consumer sales, increasing the online mix and reducing reliance on third‑party retail channels.
  • Brand and go‑to‑market: Marketing campaigns and product initiatives drove brand momentum and supported market share gains and volume improvements.
  • Operational initiatives: Completed capacity expansion and supply‑chain automation projects aimed at improving throughput and reducing lead times.

Original SEC Filing: NovaBay Pharmaceuticals, Inc. [ NBY ] - 10-K - Mar. 19, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Intuitive Machines 10-K: Revenue $210.1M, EPS (0.73)
data.description:
Intuitive Machines reported fiscal 2025 revenue of $210.1 million and a net loss per share of $(0.73), with net loss narrowing year-over-year despite pressures across operating results. Revenue declined modestly versus the prior year while operating losses widened, reflecting higher operating investments and changes in contract mix. The company continues to ramp program activity and expand capabilities through strategic acquisitions and capital investments.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenues: $210.1 million, decreased $17.9 million (‑8%) versus 2024
  • Gross Profit: $32.0 million (as reported)
  • Operating loss (Operating Income): $(87.2) million, worsened $29.8 million versus 2024
  • Net loss (Net Income): $(106.8) million, improved $240.1 million versus 2024
  • Net loss per share of Class A common stock: $(0.73) per share

Business Highlights

  • Revenue & backlog shift: Backlog declined from $328 million to $213 million driven by contract performance and timing of awards, alongside a modest YoY revenue decline.
  • Contract mix: Company shifted away from fixed-price lunar missions toward increased cost-reimbursable engineering work (OMES III, NSN) with a ramp-up in NSN activity.
  • Program milestones: Completed IM-1 in February 2024 and IM-2 in March 2025; IM-3 and IM-4 remain active with increased activity and revenue recognition for IM-4.
  • Strategic acquisitions: Acquired KinetX (October 2025) and Lanteris (January 2026) to bolster navigation, flight dynamics, and integrated spacecraft manufacturing capabilities.
  • Investment in scale and operations: Increased G&A and R&D spending to expand infrastructure, develop data relay and lunar services, and build satellite and ground network assets to support future growth.

Original SEC Filing: Intuitive Machines, Inc. [ LUNR ] - 10-K - Mar. 19, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Coeptis Therapeutics Holdings 2025 10-K: $1.36M Revenue, Operating Loss $14.23M
data.description:
Coeptis Therapeutics Holdings reported $1.363045 million in revenue for the year ended 2025, driven by NexGenAI lead-generation and webinar services, while recording operating expenses that produced an operating loss of $14.225918 million; earnings per share (EPS) and net income figures were not disclosed in the extracted sections. The results mark the company's first meaningful commercial revenue from its technology services pivot as the biotechnology programs remain development-stage. Management signaled increased R&D and general and administrative spending ahead to support advancement of its GEAR and SNAP-CAR platforms.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue (Sales): $1.363045 million — primarily from NexGenAI lead-generation and webinar services in 2025.
  • Operating Income: $(14.225918) million — operating expenses totaled $14.225918 million for the year ended 2025.
  • Net Income: Not provided in the extracted sections.
  • Net Income Per Share (EPS): Not provided in the extracted sections.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth & Business Shift: Generated $1.36 million in 2025 from NexGenAI lead-generation and webinars, representing the first meaningful commercial revenue as the company pivots toward technology services while maintaining development-focused biotech activities.
  • Product Focus & Licensing: Secured exclusive rights to the GEAR™ platform in March 2025 and holds a license from Deverra (August 2023) to expand cell therapy offerings.
  • Program Advances: Advanced CD38-GEAR-NK and CD38-Diagnostic programs; completed asset purchases and IND-related activities supporting therapeutic development.
  • Contract and Asset Changes: Terminated the SSA with Deverra in December 2024 as part of organizational and programmatic adjustments.
  • R&D and Infrastructure Outlook: Anticipates increased research and development and general and administrative expenditures to support GEAR and SNAP-CAR development and to scale operations.

Original SEC Filing: Coeptis Therapeutics Holdings, Inc. [ COEP ] - 10-K - Mar. 19, 2026

data.title: Apollo Global commits $1 billion for retail joint venture with Realty Income
data.description:
Realty Income said on Thursday it will get $1 billion from asset manager Apollo Global Management and affiliates for a 49% interest in a new joint venture that is expected to own a portfolio of single-tenant retail properties with long-term net leases.

Such retail properties are popular with investors for their steady income and resilience, as tenants typically cover property costs and often sell essential goods or services.

The deal between Realty Income and Apollo, expected to close on March 31, involves about 500 properties across diversified retail sectors including dollar stores, quick service restaurants, drug stores, grocery and health & fitness.

The commercial real estate investment trust said it will manage the properties and retain the option to buy back Apollo's stake between the 7th and 15th year, with the price set to cap the asset manager's annual return at 6.875%.

Goldman Sachs served as the financial advisor to Realty Income, and Wells Fargo did for Apollo.

data.title: Toronto Stock Exchange slips as gold, mining firms tumble
data.description:
Key points:
  • TSX falls 1.4%, down over 7% since Iran conflict began
  • Materials sector plunges 5%; gold miners drop 6% as bullion slides 3.2%
  • Small-cap gold stocks slump 8% to 12% amid broad selloff

By Purvi Agarwal and Promit Mukherjee

Canada's main stock index slipped on Thursday as an escalation in the Middle East conflict following attacks on energy infrastructure in the region added to inflation concerns, causing sharp declines in gold, silver and other mining shares.

A moderate easing of U.S. crude oil prices later in the day helped the market pare some losses.

The Toronto Stock Exchange's S&P/TSX composite index ended down 1.42% at 31,854.98, its lowest level since February, bringing the index’s total decline to over 7% since the start of the Iran war.

The materials sector , which includes gold and mining stocks, plunged by more than 5% to its lowest level this year.

Gold miners (.SPTTGD) led the decline with a drop of 6%, largely due to a rout in small gold mining companies, which fell between 8% and 12%. Spot gold prices , which scaled an all-time high of almost $5,400 per ounce at the end of January, fell 3.2% to $4,657 per ounce.

"Today ... it's all gold. The gold is crushed and tumbled," said Barry Schwartz, chief investment officer at Baskin Wealth Management.

Brent crude futures pared gains after jumping above $119 a barrel after Iran attacked energy facilities across the Middle East following Israel's strike on its South Pars gas field. Brent settled at $108.65 per barrel, up 1.2%. U.S. crude settled down slightly.

Energy stocks posted a big jump with the index up almost 3.37% and companies seeing a healthy surge in share prices. Canada is a net exporter of oil and higher prices benefit the companies and government.

"Those things (oil and mining) are notoriously very, very speculative and could move quickly," Schwartz said, adding if the Iran conflict does not last long, that could be a good buying opportunity for investors outside of the commodity sector.

Rising oil prices and no sign of ending the Iran war have eroded risk appetite, with the malaise spreading beyond the mining sector.

The index-heavyweight financial sector (.SPTTFS), industrials (.GSPTTIN) and real estate (.GSPTTRE) all lost more than a percentage point on Thursday, TSX data showed.

Royal Bank of Canada dropped by 1.21% while TD Bank shed 0.67%.

data.title: FedEx forecasts upbeat fiscal 2026 profit after solid holiday quarter, shares rise
data.description:
By Lisa Baertlein and Abhinav Parmar

FedEx , known for its air-dependent overnight deliveries, on Thursday raised its full-year profit forecast as it reported third-quarter profit and revenue above Wall Street estimates, helped by a surge in deliveries during the all-important holiday quarter.

Memphis-based FedEx, which earlier this month overtook UPS in market value for the first time, saw its shares rise 8% in after-hours trading. The company was valued at about $82.23 billion as of Wednesday's close.

FedEx now expects adjusted profit for its fiscal year ending May 31 to be between $19.30 and $20.10 per share. Analysts, on average, expect FedEx to post full-year profit of $18.69 per share, according to data compiled by LSEG.

The company in December forecast annual profit of $17.80 to $19.00 per share.

FedEx said its outlook assumes no additional geopolitical disruptions, but the fallout from the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran, which has pushed air freight rates higher and forced carriers to reroute flights, could weigh on fourth-quarter performance.

About 8% of FedEx's international export volume flows through hubs in the region, Stifel analysts said, highlighting its exposure to ongoing disruption.

FedEx said operating results in its Express unit improved in the third quarter, helped by stronger U.S. and international package pricing, higher domestic volumes and ongoing cost cuts.

The third-quarter "results were lifted by much higher yields, but this time much stronger U.S. ground volume also helped the top line," Evercore ISI analyst Jonathan Chappell said.

"The cost savings from the network reorganization also continue to help expand margins, and all 3 added up to a very surprising beat," he added.

The company said that gains were partly offset by higher wages and incentive pay, rising transportation costs, the impact of global trade policy changes, and the grounding of MD-11 aircraft.

Adjusted earnings for its crucial winter holiday quarter rose to $5.25 per share, beating analysts' estimates of $4.14 per share, even as it absorbed millions in unexpected costs related to truck and plane replacements for its MD-11 fleet, which was grounded after a deadly UPS crash in November 2025.

FedEx had 28 Boeing MD-11 cargo jets in operation when the Federal Aviation Administration grounded the planes after the crash that killed 14 people, including three pilots on board.

Executives previously said they expect the company's MD-11 fleet to return to service by the end of May.

The company also said that it now expects its full-year revenue to be up in the range of 6.0% to 6.5% year-over-year, compared to its previous forecast of growth between 5% and 6%.

FedEx is in a multi-year restructuring that includes slashing billions of dollars in costs, combining its distinct Ground and Express delivery options, automating some operations and spinning off its Freight trucking business on June 1.

FedEx posted revenue for the quarter ended February 28 of $24 billion, above analysts' estimates of $23.43 billion.
